data.title: check-skill-refs.sh: no code-span exemption -> backtick-wrapped skill names flagged as unqualified refs
data.description:
## Expected Behavior
Skill names wrapped in backtick code spans (e.g., `/sprint`, `/brainstorm`) used as negative examples in documentation should be exempt from the unqualified-reference check, since they are illustrating what NOT to write rather than actually referencing a skill.

## Actual Behavior
check-skill-refs.sh uses a perl scanner that strips URLs but has no exemption for inline code spans or fenced code blocks. Any line containing `/sprint` (even wrapped in backticks) is flagged as an unqualified skill reference.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Add a line to any file in the scan set (skills/, docs/, hooks/, commands/, CLAUDE.md):
   e.g., `Short-form references like `/sprint` are invalid`
2. Run `bash plugins/dso/scripts/check-skill-refs.sh`
3. Observe false positive: UNQUALIFIED: file:line: ...

## Workaround Applied
In onboarding/SKILL.md commit 3801f702, replaced concrete skill names in the negative example with a generic placeholder `/<skill-name>` to avoid triggering the linter. This workaround degrades the documentation quality — readers see a generic placeholder rather than a concrete example of the anti-pattern.

## Fix Direction
Add a pre-processing step to the perl scanner that strips inline code spans (`...`) and fenced code blocks (```...```) before applying the unqualified-reference pattern. Alternatively, support a per-line suppression annotation (e.g., `# skill-refs-ok`).
